what_it_does: Defines window.MS_DECISIONS, a typed declarative catalog of open design decisions for a studio UI. Each decision maps to an existing tweak key (e.g. radius, density, warmth, accent, motion, voice, navStyle, wallTexture, candidates, zoning) and lists options with values, descriptions, and recommended flags; it also provides categories, ordering, preview hints, affected token lists, and helper functions to list, retrieve, categorize, resolve active/recommended options, and generate radius CSS variables from a multiplier.
observations: Decisions are a thin descriptive layer over existing tweak keys; committing an option is intended to call setTweak(decision.tweak, option.value).
CATEGORIES defines ordered grouping with id, label, and blurb; ORDER controls presentation sequence independently of DECISIONS object keys.
RADIUS_BASE and radiusVars centralize the --r-* ramp multiplier logic used by the radius decision.
Each decision includes metadata such as title, question, why, affects, tweak, preview, and an options array.
Option values mix numbers, strings, and booleans; some options carry extra fields like curve for motion or sample for voice.
get() logs a loud error for unknown ids, while has() provides a silent existence check.
activeOption resolves a live tweak value to an option using nearest-neighbor matching for numbers and exact equality otherwise.
byCategory builds a map from category ids to decision arrays, seeded from CATEGORIES.
recommended() falls back to the first option when no option is marked recommended.
The file exposes _decisions as a private-ish lookup alongside the public API.
standouts: Design decisions reuse the existing tweak system rather than introducing a parallel preference store.
radiusVars is the only decision-specific computation helper, turning a scalar into a full CSS variable ramp.
activeOption implements numeric nearest-neighbor resolution so live tweak values map back to the closest labeled option.
The voice decision includes sample copy per option, making the choice concrete without extra UI text.
conventions: Uses an IIFE to expose a single global window.MS_DECISIONS namespace.
Separates immutable data tables (CATEGORIES, DECISIONS, ORDER, RADIUS_BASE) from accessor functions.
Uses Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call for the silent has() existence check.
Comments document cross-file relationships and architectural intent (app.jsx, colors_and_type.css).
Option ids are short slugs while labels are human-readable.
Recommended options are flagged explicitly rather than inferred from position.
concerns: activeOption uses the type of options[0].value to decide numeric vs exact matching; a decision with mixed option value types would behave incorrectly.
radiusVars appends 'px' to toFixed(1) output, which can produce values like '1.0px' and may need parsing on the consumer side.
DECISIONS ids are duplicated as object keys and inside each decision.id, plus ORDER; keeping them in sync is manual.
categoryLabel logs an error but then silently returns the raw id as a fallback.
Several decisions use preview:'label', which offers less guidance than dedicated specimen renderers.
notes: This file is the data catalog behind a design-decision workspace: it describes each studio design choice, maps it to an existing tweak key, and provides helpers to present options and resolve the live selection.
questions: Where is setTweak defined and how are tweak values persisted and applied in app.jsx?
How does the workspace interpret the preview strings (e.g. 'radius', 'density', 'accent', 'motion', 'voice', 'label')?
Are voice samples localized, or are they always the English strings shown here?
Is ORDER static, or is it intended to be user-customizable?
How does the workspace handle a tweak value that does not match any option exactly?
Are the recommended flags used only as fallbacks, or do they also drive UI badges and default highlighting?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches implementation: it is a descriptive catalog that maps decisions to existing tweak keys without introducing a parallel store.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for design tokens and preferences.
Human-readable design decisions over raw control values.
Consistency through multipliers and derived variable ramps.
Curated defaults with explicit recommended options.
Separation of data model from presentation order and categorization.
intention_signals: Provide a curated, explainable design-system configuration surface where each high-level choice is backed by a single existing tweak, enabling a workspace UI to guide users through coherent design decisions without duplicating state.
novelty: Treats design decisions as a first-class declarative catalog with rationale, affected tokens, preview hints, and nearest-value resolution, rather than exposing raw tweak keys directly.
contribution: A structured, self-describing decision catalog and accessor API that a fused system can reuse to drive a guided design workspace; it keeps decision metadata, category ordering, option resolution, and radius variable generation in one place.
summary_for_embedding: A JavaScript IIFE that exposes window.MS_DECISIONS, a declarative catalog of studio design decisions mapping each decision and its options to existing tweak keys, with categories, preview hints, recommended-option resolution, and helper functions for listing, categorizing, and computing CSS radius variables.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a curated, explainable design-decision workspace surface that reuses the existing tweak system instead of introducing a parallel preference store.

symbols.does: Generates a CSS variable map by multiplying a base radius ramp by a scalar.
symbols.description: Takes a multiplier k and returns an object mapping each --r-* token from RADIUS_BASE to a pixel string (e.g. '8.0px'), mirroring the radius ramp in colors_and_type.css and the model used by app.jsx.

symbols.does: Returns the decisions in presentation order.
symbols.description: Maps the ORDER array over DECISIONS to produce an array of decision objects, enforcing the curated sequence in which the workspace should present choices.

symbols.does: Retrieves a decision by id, logging an error if unknown.
symbols.description: Looks up a decision in DECISIONS by id and returns it; if the id is not recognized, it logs a loud error listing known decision ids and returns null.

symbols.does: Silently checks whether a given id is a known decision.
symbols.description: Uses Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call to test DECISIONS without logging, intended for callers that only need to know whether a key maps to a design decision.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the category definitions.
symbols.description: Returns a copy of the CATEGORIES array containing ids, labels, and blurbs for the decision grouping used by the workspace.

symbols.does: Returns the human-readable label for a category id.
symbols.description: Looks up a category by id and returns its label; logs an error and falls back to the raw id if the category is unknown.

symbols.does: Groups all decisions under their category ids.
symbols.description: Builds a map keyed by category id, initialized from CATEGORIES, then places each decision from ORDER into its corresponding category array.

symbols.does: Finds an option within a decision by option id.
symbols.description: Searches a decision's options array for the given option id; logs an error and returns null if no matching option exists.

symbols.does: Returns the recommended option for a decision, defaulting to the first option.
symbols.description: Selects the first option marked recommended, or the first option if none is marked, providing the default value when no live tweak is set.

symbols.does: Determines which option matches the current tweak value.
symbols.description: Compares the live tweak value against a decision's options; for numeric option values it picks the nearest match, otherwise it requires an exact value match, falling back to the recommended option.
